description: PRD-6 Team Management & User Identity. Four stories: (1) extract and test a getInitials pure function, (2) fix the broken getMembersForProject two-table join bug with a multi-step query, (3) replace the hardcoded user avatar with a real UserMenu dropdown (initials, theme toggle, Manage Team link, sign out), (4) gate the invite form behind an OCA role check. No new migrations. All changes are frontend-only.

userStories.title: getInitials pure function + identity lib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a tested pure function that derives display initials from a user's full name or email so that every component that renders a user avatar uses consistent, correct logic.
acceptanceCriteria: New file frontend/src/lib/identity.ts exports getInitials(fullName: string | undefined, email: string): string
Two-word name returns first letter of each word uppercased (e.g. 'John Smith' → 'JS')
Single-word name returns its first letter uppercased (e.g. 'Alice' → 'A')
Three-or-more-word name returns first letter of first word + first letter of last word uppercased (e.g. 'Mary Jane Watson' → 'MW')
undefined fullName falls back to first letter of email uppercased
Empty string fullName falls back to first letter of email uppercased
New test file frontend/src/lib/__tests__/identity.test.ts covers all five cases above
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix getMembersForProject — two-step query with org isolation
userStories.description: As a project OCA viewing the members page, I want the member list to show correct roles so that I can trust who is an OCA versus a CX engineer on this project.
acceptanceCriteria: getMembersForProject in frontend/src/lib/members.ts is rewritten to use six sequential Supabase queries joined in JS: (1) participations where project_id=X → user_ids, (2) projects where id=X → org_id, (3) users where id IN(user_ids) → email+full_name, (4) memberships where user_id IN(user_ids) AND org_id=org_id → roles, (5) discipline_scopes where project_id=X → scope ids, (6) assignments where user_id IN(user_ids) AND discipline_scope_id IN(scope_ids) → discipline names
The function returns early with [] if participations is empty (skip the remaining five queries)
ProjectMember type gains optional full_name?: string field
Existing tests in frontend/src/lib/__tests__/members.test.ts are updated to mock the new multi-table call pattern
New test verifies org isolation: a user who is a member of org-A AND org-B gets their org-A role (not org-B role) when querying an org-A project
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: UserMenu dropdown — initials avatar, theme toggle, Manage Team, Sign Out
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a dropdown from my avatar that lets me toggle the theme, navigate to team management, and sign out so that I don't have to hunt through the sidebar for these actions.
acceptanceCriteria: New component frontend/src/components/UserMenu.tsx created
UserMenu fetches the current user via supabase.auth.getUser() on mount and reads full_name from user_metadata (field name: full_name) and falls back to email
Avatar circle displays initials via getInitials() imported from @/lib/identity
Clicking the avatar toggles a dropdown; clicking outside the dropdown closes it (use a useEffect + mousedown listener on document)
Dropdown item 1: theme toggle button — clicking it toggles between light and dark, updates data-theme on documentElement and localStorage (same logic that was in the sidebar bp-nav-bottom button)
Dropdown item 2: 'Manage Team' link rendered as a Next.js Link to /project/{projectId}/members — only rendered when projectId prop is non-null; hidden otherwise
Dropdown item 3: 'Sign Out' button — calls supabase.auth.signOut() then router.push('/auth')
frontend/src/app/(app)/layout.tsx replaces the bp-user div with <UserMenu projectId={projectId} theme={theme} onThemeChange={setTheme} /> passing down theme state
The bp-nav-bottom div and its theme toggle button are removed from layout.tsx sidebar
All new elements use bp-* CSS class names consistent with the existing design system (no hardcoded Tailwind color classes like bg-blue-600)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: avatar shows correct initials, dropdown opens and closes, theme toggle switches theme, sign out redirects to /auth

userStories.title: OCA gate on invite form + getCurrentUserRole function
userStories.description: As a CX engineer viewing the members page, I want the invite form hidden so that I cannot accidentally attempt an action I don't have permission to perform.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/members.ts exports a new function getCurrentUserRole(userId: string, projectId: string): Promise<'OCA' | 'cx_engineer' | null>
getCurrentUserRole fetches org_id from projects where id=projectId, then fetches role from memberships where user_id=userId AND org_id=org_id, returns the role string or null if no row found

New tests in frontend/src/lib/__tests__/members.test.ts cover getCurrentUserRole: returns 'OCA' for an OCA member, returns 'cx_engineer' for a regular member, returns null when the user has no membership in the project's org
frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx calls getCurrentUserRole(session.user.id, projectId) after the session check and stores the result in an isOCA boolean state
The 'Invite Team Member' form section is only rendered when isOCA is true
When isOCA is false, a read-only notice is rendered in place of the form: 'Only OCAs can invite members'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: sign in as a cx_engineer user, navigate to a project members page, confirm the invite form is absent and the notice is shown
